rulings.Case Title: JOANNE SETTERBERG et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. DEFENDANT NIBCO INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Off calendar per the dismissal of moving party filed on January 6, 2010. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE EGGLETON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs Irene Eggleton, et al.'s motion for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. Ms. Eggleton's request that the second amended complaint be deemed served on all defendants who have previously appeared is granted on the condition that she serve any defendant who requests service of the second amended complaint. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN AYER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant General Cable Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. General Cable failed to satisfy its burden of production because it did not present with its moving papers plaintiff's verified substantive responses to contention interrogatories showing that plaintiff has no evidence and cannot reasonably obtain any evidence supporting his claims. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN SOTELO VS. AMCORD, INC.(INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN et al

rulings.Case Title: JAMES NASH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. DEFENDANT LEHIGH SOUTHWEST CEMENT COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is off calendar per request of moving party. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LAWRENCE LEVENE VS. BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, February 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant McCord Corporation's demurrer is moot as Mr. Lawrence's claims for false representation and intentional tort/intentional failure to warn were dismissed on January 29, 2010. =(220/HEK)
